programs.program_course_name: Auckland Law School – Melbourne Law School Dual Masters (LLM) Programme

programs.academic_requirements: International Baccalaureate Diploma with a minimum total score of 24; General Certificate of Education (GCE) Advanced Level with a minimum grade of CCC in three subjects; or one year successful bachelor-degree level study at a recognised university overseas. IELTS Academic score of 6.0 overall, no band below 5.5.
programs.india_qualification: Indian equivalent: International Baccalaureate Diploma with a minimum total score of 24; General Certificate of Education (GCE) Advanced Level with a minimum grade of CCC in three subjects; or one year successful bachelor-degree level study at a recognised university overseas. IELTS Academic score of 6.0 overall, no band below 5.5.. Indian students should provide transcripts from a recognised Indian university or board (CBSE/ICSE/State board) showing the equivalent standard.

GPA/GPE below 3.0 recommended to start in Postgraduate Certificate in Health Sciences as a pathway towards PGDipHSc.. Indian students should provide transcripts from a recognised Indian university or board (CBSE/ICSE/State board) showing the equivalent standard.

programs.academic_requirements: A Bachelors degree in a relevant subject with a Grade Point Average of 3.0 or higher in 60 points above Stage IIA Bachelor (Honours) degree in a relevant subject with a Grade Point Average of 3.0 or higher in 60 points above Stage III, and completed an introductory programming course in MATLAB, C, Python, or similar coding language, and an advanced undergraduate course on partial differential equations
programs.india_qualification: Indian equivalent: A Bachelors degree in a relevant subject with a Grade Point Average of 3.0 or higher in 60 points above Stage IIA Bachelor (Honours) degree in a relevant subject with a Grade Point Average of 3.0 or higher in 60 points above Stage III, and completed an introductory programming course in MATLAB, C, Python, or similar coding language, and an advanced undergraduate course on partial differential equations. Indian students should provide transcripts from a recognised Indian university or board (CBSE/ICSE/State board) showing the equivalent standard.
